page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores using the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It operates without hyperthreading, providing exactly one thread per core. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. The server is provisioned with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the server's exceptional memory subsystem performance. It achieves top-tier status in large-block memory bandwidth tests for both read and write operations, as well as in Passmark memory and database operation benchmarks. Geekbench multi-core performance also ranks in the top tier. Single-core processing, software compilation, and text processing show strong performance in the top 25 percent. In contrast, LLM inference speeds vary, showing average to weak performance for prompt processing on larger models, and poor performance for text generation on small models, though it maintains strong text generation speeds on medium models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.8xlarge leverages the ARM64 Graviton architecture to deliver high memory density and memory bandwidth without the overhead of x86 licensing. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its massive memory capacity, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier database benchmark results, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading, supported by 256.0 GB of RAM, which provides a high ratio of 8.0 GB of memory per core. The server includes a 67 Gbps baseline network interface, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the r8gb.8xlarge delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is particularly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving workloads that benefit from high memory density and rapid memory access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 server delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high-density RAM for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database benchmark performance
67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and web serving
